metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn — a mid-session model switch emits a second <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> block the parser harvests, flipping ModelName to model B (latest-wins); the driver still stubs wait_turn, so record ports the turn-done poll first (route driver-gap).

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes** — `agy` lets the user change the active model mid-session. Each change writes a `<USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>` block (`changed setting \`Model Selection\` from <A> to <B>.`) into the next USER_INPUT step's content — the exact shape `TestModelExtraction` asserts (`from Gemini 2.0 to Claude Opus 4.1`).

**daemon=full** — The parser harvests the model from that block and attaches it to subsequent assistant events; a second switch lands a fresh model string. MergeMetrics' latest-non-unknown-wins rule promotes `ModelName` to model B by turn 2. So turn-1-model-A → turn-2-model-B is observable from the tailed transcript. (PROVISIONAL caveat: the v1 parser harvests the model ONLY when `p.model == ""` — i.e. the FIRST settings-change seen. A SECOND mid-session switch may NOT update p.model under the current `if p.model == ""` guard, so the recorded ModelName could stay pinned to model A even though agy switched. This is the load-bearing unknown record must settle: if the second switch does not flip ModelName, this becomes daemon=bug (the parser ignores later switches) with a known_failing spec. Assessed daemon=full provisionally because the transcript DOES carry the second switch — the signal reaches the Source; whether the parser promotes it is what record verifies.)

**driver=gap:wait_turn** — Needs `wait_turn` between turns (stubbed) plus the model switch as a `slash`/`keys` step. Verify whether agy's model switch is an inline slash (`/model <id>` → `slash` step) or an arrow-key picker (`keys` step) during record; the driver stubs both `wait_turn` and `keys`. record ports the turn-done poll + the switch primitive first.

Observations: `model` provisional (record fills both A and B from the recording); cost/tokens omitted (SQLite-only).
caveats: POTENTIAL daemon=bug: parser harvests model only while p.model=='' (first switch); a SECOND mid-session switch may not flip ModelName — record settles full vs bug and known_failing.
Switch mechanism (inline slash vs arrow-key picker) unconfirmed; may make this driver=gap:keys in addition to wait_turn.
cost/tokens observations omitted: usage is SQLite-protobuf only.

sources.note: parseUserInput harvests model only when p.model=='' — a second switch may not update it (the verdict's key risk)
TestModelExtraction covers 'from Gemini 2.0 to Claude Opus 4.1' — the mid-session switch shape
sibling: agent=partial because the recorded model did not flip under API-key auth — analogous live risk

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
